reason: The item cleanly targets mechanism-level limits of common cell safety devices across abuse modes, with correct choices bounded to specific, non-universal claims (e.g., spatial/temporal lag and irreversibility) rather than vague safety generalities. Decoys are mostly single-fault and engineering-plausible, requiring experts to avoid threshold-ordering and “complete protection” heuristics, though one decoy risks being too obviously wrong via absolute language and thus slightly reduces discriminative strength.

The item cleanly targets boundary-aware engineering judgment about why a small-stack thermal-mass mitigation result cannot be naively scaled to a full pack, with two distinct, well-scoped failure channels (configuration-specific propagation threshold vs. system-level mass/volume/cost constraints). Its discriminative power is high because it penalizes single-factor thinking (plate thickness alone) and forces the solver to articulate mechanisms rather than recite outcomes, though scoring may vary somewhat across raters due to open-ended phrasing and the possibility of additional valid non-gold reasons being offered.
